note: AUD5 fix for finding F-17 (docs/flowmap/audit/04-findings.md). SCOPE: test-authoring — the AUD3-missing smoke tests ARE the fix (T10).

intent.problem: AUD3 T10: onboard.mjs — the B1 door whose whole contract is 'exit 0 = trustworthy, 1 = NOT' — had no deny test; the refusal path was never exercised.
intent.approach: onboard.test.mjs: an isolated git worktree at HEAD (node_modules symlinked in) with one fragment deleted — onboard must exit 1, print 'STOP — the map is NOT trustworthy' AND name the exact uncovered file (right-reason pinned, not just any failure).
intent.rationale: a worktree is the only honest fixture for a tool that verifies 'this checkout'; symlinking node_modules is the documented H4 boundary workaround.

intent.problem: AUD3 T10: status.mjs (C1 — 'continuity is derived state') had only the thin exit∈{0,3} probe in loop-e2e; no test asserted WHICH verdict a change gets.
intent.approach: status.test.mjs (in the suite): one fixture plan against the real code proves pending (unimplemented add), built (structure-only modify of a real node) and drifted (wrong proposed signature) simultaneously with exit 3; a second proves all-built exit 0 and no-plan exit 2.
intent.rationale: built/pending/drifted are the resumption contract a 0-context session trusts; locking them on the real ts-morph machinery (not mocks) is what makes 'derived state' provable.
